topic: El Gordo cluster impossibility — post-integration adversarial pass on Self-Contradicted verdict
change_summary: Drift audit head non-OBE candidate after WIN-052 (which was OBE on c8). WIN-054 priority_bucket=verdict_changed: verdict flipped Not Demonstrated -> Self-Contradicted via EXP-295-P4 since c3 (2026-05-09). detail_verdict_length grew 11.7% (2207 -> 2466). Compared on common fields only (c3 fingerprint is pre-TLDR-schema). sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-16T01:10:33Z; obe_skipped=1 (WIN-052 c8 already re-reviewed 2026-05-17T10:46Z). priority-queue head (queue_id 378, WIN-070) is strict-match covered by WIN-070.c4.json, no human notes pending, no critical/strategic poller-change audit pending.
our_argument_summary: WIN-054 (El Gordo cluster impossibility) currently sits at verdict Self-Contradicted after EXP-295-P4 integration. The detail_verdict_text now correctly anchors on the bounded-firmament foreclosure horn (data/wins.json:054 detail_verdict_text: "'Self-Contradicted' is the correct verdict: the dome's own bounded geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km — a disc diameter of 40,030 km) forecloses the spatial framework in which El Gordo at z=0.87 (~1.5 Gpc, ~4.6×10²⁵ m) can exist — eighteen orders of magnitude beyond the dome's maximum extent"), and the tldr_verdict has been rewritten to drop the false 'detail prose already presents this dilemma' attribution (c3 hole #2). The c3 sub_rationale dead-pointer was also dropped (ISS-2025 fixed 2026-05-16T17:24Z; ISS-2038 wontfix as dup). Three of the four c3 blocking/moderate holes are RESOLVED. However, two NEW post-integration findings emerge from the verdict flip: (1) data/wins.json:054 finding still ends with the verbatim sentence 'Correctly acknowledging a competitor's unresolved problem while providing no dome-specific prediction is Not Demonstrated.' — the same class of verdict-versus-prose self-anchor that c3 hole #1 caught in detail_verdict_text, now living in the summary-table row (the most-read field in the WIN); (2) build-scripts/generate-html.js:1726 (rendered to docs/index.html:645) contains the hardcoded methodology sentence 'Pure false-dilemma — correctly identifying a genuine anomaly without offering dome physics — is Not Demonstrated rather than Misleading; WIN-054 (El Gordo) is the reference case for this distinction.' EXP-295-P4 patched the WIN's verdict but did not propagate the change to this cross-page methodology pointer. Both are verdict-versus-prose internal contradictions introduced by the verdict flip; neither was flagged in EXP-295 or in c3 because both surfaces are downstream of the patched fields.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (not re-examined — c2/c3 baseline carried forward; no monitoring domain exists for any cosmological WIN)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automated calculation. WIN-054 has monitoring=none. No monitor.py domain tracks El Gordo, galaxy clusters, or cosmological structure formation.
data_sources_used: Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023 ApJ 954 162 (6.2sigma tension) — doi 10.3847/1538-4357/ace62a
Menanteau et al. 2012 ApJ 753 163 (initial discovery) — doi 10.1088/0004-637X/753/2/163
Asencio et al. 2021 MNRAS 500 5249 (initial mass) — doi 10.1093/mnras/staa3441
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None at the code level (no automation). At the prose level, the dome's 'no expansion epoch; aetheric condensation on thermal timescale; high-velocity collisions natural' remains a bare assertion with no equations — flagged correctly in detail_evidence.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new this cycle. Code tags unchanged from c3.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c3. WIN-054 has no monitor.py domain (data/wins.json:054 detail_evidence: 'no monitoring infrastructure (zero of the 39 monitor.py domains track cosmological claims)'). Zero automated validation. The verdict flip from Not Demonstrated to Self-Contradicted does not require revisiting code tags — it is a label and rationale-shape change, not a discovery of new dome automation.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c3. El Gordo is an empirical anomaly (Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023 flag a 6.2σ ΛCDM tension), not a standard-physics prediction the dome is relabeling. The dome is hijacking a published anomaly, not renaming an established mechanism.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Kim+ 2021, Asencio+ 2023, Menanteau+ 2012 all predate the dome's V51.0 import by years to a decade. The detail_verdict_text continues to run a parallel post-hoc-test failure mode (independent of the SC argument). NOTE: under the Self-Contradicted verdict the post_hoc tag remains accurate (the dome adopted the published value), but it is no longer the primary verdict driver — SC pattern (2) (foreclosure-by-own-commitments) is the load-bearing argument. Tag stays true; rationale slightly shifts from primary to secondary failure mode.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Zero derivation chain from dome geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km) to El Gordo's M_200c=2.13e15 M_sun or V_infall>=2,500 km/s. The dome's 'aetheric condensation' accommodation is acknowledged in detail_evidence as having no equations or mass function.

holes_found.description: VERDICT-VERSUS-FINDING SELF-ANCHOR: data/wins.json:054 finding (the summary-table row text, the most prominent field a skim reader sees) still ends with the verbatim sentence 'Correctly acknowledging a competitor's unresolved problem while providing no dome-specific prediction is Not Demonstrated.' This is the same class of bug c3 hole #1 caught in detail_verdict_text — a prior-verdict self-anchor left in place when the verdict label was flipped — except this one lives in finding, which renders in the Part 3 summary table row alongside the verdict cell. A skim reader sees 'Self-Contradicted' in column three and 'is Not Demonstrated' at the end of column four on the same row. EXP-295-P4 patches[4] explicitly scoped finding as out-of-scope (per c3 review of EXP-295 scope_notes: 'only verdict + tldr_verdict are patched, detail prose preserved as-is'), and the post-integration detail_verdict_text fix that resolved c3 hole #1 did not propagate to finding. Verified by direct read of data/wins.json:054 after git pull --rebase: finding still 427 chars, ends 'is Not Demonstrated.' verbatim. Verified by grep on monitor/decisions/open-issues.json: zero open issues mention 'Not Demonstrated' AND '054', so this is not already-tracked. Severity major (not critical) because the SC argument elsewhere in the WIN holds — but the summary-table mismatch is exactly the surface a dome defender (or skim auditor) will use to claim 'your own review says it's Not Demonstrated.'
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should patch data/wins.json:054 finding to remove the 'is Not Demonstrated' self-anchor. Suggested replacement (matches the rhetoric shape of the now-canonical SC-anchored detail_verdict_text): 'El Gordo is a genuine 6.2σ ΛCDM tension that remains unresolved (Asencio et al. 2023). The dome correctly identifies this real challenge but its own bounded geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km) forecloses the spatial framework in which El Gordo at z=0.87 (~1.5 Gpc, ~4.6×10²⁵ m) can exist. The WIN cites that very object as confirming evidence while the dome's own commitments deny the spatial framework the citation requires.' This preserves length (~440 chars, comparable to current 427), preserves the credit-where-due framing, and aligns the summary-table row with the verdict cell on the same row. Class hint: verification (the SC argument and the supporting numerics are already in detail_verdict_text — finding is just being aligned with what the WIN now is).

holes_found.description: CROSS-PAGE METHODOLOGY-VERSUS-VERDICT MISMATCH: build-scripts/generate-html.js:1726 (hardcoded in the build script, rendered to docs/index.html:645) contains the methodology sentence 'Pure false-dilemma — correctly identifying a genuine anomaly without offering dome physics — is Not Demonstrated rather than Misleading; WIN-054 (El Gordo) is the reference case for this distinction.' This sentence explicitly names WIN-054 as the worked exemplar of the Not Demonstrated verdict category in Part 1 (Verdict Assignment Criteria, eg-verdicts section). After the EXP-295-P4 verdict flip, WIN-054 is Self-Contradicted, not Not Demonstrated — but Part 1 still tells the reader 'WIN-054 is the reference case' for Not Demonstrated. A reader who follows the cross-reference from Part 1 to WIN-054 will hit the verdict-versus-methodology mismatch immediately. EXP-295-P4 patched the WIN itself but did not propagate the change to this downstream methodology pointer. Source confirmation: grep on the live clone, build-scripts/generate-html.js line 1726 contains the verbatim text; same text appears in docs/index.html line 645; same text appears in monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-155.json:68 as the original analyst-authored phrasing. This is an architecture-debt finding (hardcoded prose in build-scripts/generate-html.js is itself a smell — the c3 sub_rationale finding called out the same architecture problem for a different field; ISS-2025 fixed the dead pointer without fixing the underlying 'hardcoded prose in generate-html.js' pattern). Severity major because a reader navigating from Part 1 verdict taxonomy to the actual WIN encounters an explicit name-mismatch. Not critical because the local SC argument at WIN-054 itself is internally consistent and reads cleanly in isolation.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should do ONE of: (a) update build-scripts/generate-html.js:1726 to either replace WIN-054 with a different reference case (search wins.json for a different Not Demonstrated verdict — current count of Not Demonstrated WINs: see live data — pick the clearest pure-false-dilemma case) or rewrite the sentence to drop the reference-case anchor entirely; (b) move the methodology prose out of build-scripts/generate-html.js and into data/sections.json:part1.html with a similar fix, addressing both the immediate mismatch and the architecture issue at the same time; (c) wontfix-with-rationale only if the decider can articulate why a Not-Demonstrated reference-case-named WIN-054 is acceptable when the WIN itself is now Self-Contradicted (no such rationale is apparent — the sentence as written is straightforwardly wrong post-flip). Option (a) is the minimum fix and is consistent with how ISS-2025 was handled (drop the dead pointer); option (b) is the better long-term fix. Class hint: deep-attack (a Not Demonstrated reference case needs to be selected, which is a substantive content choice, not a verification).

holes_found.description: ORDERS-OF-MAGNITUDE NUMERIC SLIGHTLY UNDERSTATED: The detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict claim 'eighteen orders of magnitude beyond the dome's maximum extent.' Computed live this run (node integration of Hogg 1999 distance formula at z=0.87 with ΛCDM H_0=70, Ω_M=0.3, Ω_Λ=0.7): angular-diameter distance dA = 1591 Mpc = 4.91×10²⁵ m; ratio dA/firmament_height = 5.40×10¹⁸; log10(ratio) = 18.73. So 'eighteen' is correct if rounded down but 'nineteen' if rounded to nearest. Using comoving distance instead (a perfectly defensible choice for 'where the cluster is' under standard cosmology): dC = 2975 Mpc = 9.18×10²⁵ m; ratio dC/firmament = 1.01×10¹⁹; log10 = 19.00 exactly. The detail_verdict_text rounds to 'eighteen' from the dA reading, which is conservative but understates a defender-resistant numeric. Also: detail_verdict_text says '~4.6×10²⁵ m' for the angular-diameter distance; computed 4.91×10²⁵ m — within 6%, acceptable for an order-of-magnitude argument but a defender could nitpick the 6% gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional sharpening: rephrase 'eighteen orders of magnitude' to 'more than eighteen orders of magnitude' or 'nearly nineteen orders of magnitude' in both detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict, and update the dimensional estimate to '~4.9×10²⁵ m' to match the computed value within rounding. Both fixes are <5 chars each and preserve the SC anchor while making the numeric defender-resistant. Not blocking — the SC argument lands at any rounding within the 18-19 OOM range. Class hint: verification (a small numeric tightening, not a content change).

holes_found.description: LAMBDA_A SECOND SC HORN STILL NOT SURFACED IN VERDICT RATIONALE: detail_evidence mentions 'The sole cosmological parameter is an unexplained λ<sub>A</sub> = 4,283 Mpc redshift scale with no derivation' but treats this only as evidence of the dome's missing-physics gap. The c3 stronger_arguments[1] proposed surfacing λ_A=4283 Mpc as a SECOND internal SC horn: this parameter, published in the dome's own unified-theory tables, corresponds to 1.32×10²⁶ m, which exceeds the dome's published disc_radius=20,015 km = 2.00×10⁷ m by ~6.6×10¹⁸ (log10 = 18.82). That is the dome publishing a cosmological-scale length parameter that is itself ~19 orders of magnitude larger than its own bounded firmament — a dome-internal contradiction independent of the El Gordo argument. EXP-295-P4 did not incorporate this. The current detail_verdict_text leans entirely on the El Gordo-distance horn. Surfacing λ_A would give WIN-054 two independent SC horns (both dome-internal: bounded geometry vs. cited extragalactic scale; published parameter vs. published bound), strengthening the SC reading.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional enhancement: add a sentence to detail_verdict_text after the existing foreclosure argument, something like 'The dome's own cosmological-scale parameter λ_A = 4,283 Mpc = 1.32×10²⁶ m (per its unified-theory table) exceeds the published disc_radius = 20,015 km = 2.00×10⁷ m by ~6.6×10¹⁸ — a second internal contradiction in which the dome publishes a cosmological scale parameter ~19 orders of magnitude larger than the bound it sets on its own spatial extent.' Class hint: deep-attack (genuine new argument content, not a verification of existing prose). Not blocking; the single-horn SC argument lands. WIN-052 already has the parallel observation at stronger_arguments — keeping the WIN-052 / WIN-054 framing symmetric on this point would also be helpful.

holes_found.description: ILLUSORY-PROJECTION DEFENSE PARTIALLY PREEMPTED BUT NOT EXPLICITLY: A dome defender's strongest single rebuttal of the SC reclassification is: 'The dome does not commit to El Gordo's physical existence at the standard-cosmology distance. Cosmological objects are interpretive constructs within mainstream cosmology; the WIN cites a tension that disqualifies LCDM without endorsing LCDM's interpretive frame as physically real. Therefore no self-contradiction.' The current detail_verdict_text addresses this implicitly via 'The dome cites El Gordo as confirming evidence while its own commitments deny the spatial framework that citation requires,' but never explicitly engages the 'cite-without-endorsing' move. A reader sympathetic to the defender's framing could read past this and not see the dilemma made explicit. detail_evidence does have the 'silence' framing ('That is not accommodation — it is silence') which engages a related point but does not directly close the cite-without-endorsing horn.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional sharpening: add one sentence to detail_verdict_text after the foreclosure paragraph: 'The defense that the WIN cites El Gordo's measurements without endorsing standard cosmology's interpretive frame fails on its own terms — a measurement of mass and velocity at z=0.87 is meaningless under any framework that denies the spatial scale at which those quantities are defined; the WIN cannot import the numerical content of the citation while disowning the spatial framework that gives the numbers meaning.' Closes the cite-without-endorsing horn explicitly. Not blocking; the SC argument holds without this. Class hint: verification (strengthens existing argument without changing it).

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER CHECK on c3 holes: all four blocking/moderate c3 holes are RESOLVED in the integrated state. (a) c3 hole #1 (detail_verdict_text 'Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict' verbatim self-anchor) — RESOLVED: current detail_verdict_text says "'Self-Contradicted' is the correct verdict" verbatim, and the prior offending sentence is gone. (b) c3 hole #2 (tldr_verdict false 'detail prose already presents this dilemma' attribution) — RESOLVED: current tldr_verdict drops the false claim and anchors on the ~10^19 dimensional gap directly. (c) c3 hole #3 (sub_rationale field not wired into build pipeline + dead Part 6.13 pointer) — RESOLVED via ISS-2025 fixed 2026-05-16T17:24Z by decider-bau-self-apply (verified clean by verify-pending-2026-05-16T1823Z-12; dead pointer dropped from all five WINs in the EXP-295 cohort). ISS-2038 closed wontfix as duplicate of ISS-2025. (d) c3 hole #4 (detail_verdict_text absence-of-physics rhetoric mismatch with SC pattern (2)) — RESOLVED: current detail_verdict_text leads with the foreclosure argument as primary, with the absence-of-physics critique demoted to a secondary point. (e) c3 hole #5 (minor tldr drops credit-where-due framing) — PARTIALLY PRESERVED: tldr_evidence still has 'We give the dome credit for citing a genuine, peer-reviewed tension,' though tldr_verdict itself is more confrontational. Acceptable. (f) c3 hole #6 (Part 6.13 spot check) — N/A informational. No carry-over entries needed for unresolved_prior_cycle (all c3 holes either closed or this run's downstream holes are genuinely fresh post-integration findings, not restatements).
holes_found.recommendation: No action required. Recording for audit trail. The post-integration adversarial review surfaced two new majors (finding self-anchor; cross-page reference case) that EXP-295 / c3 did not catch because both surfaces are downstream of the patched fields. This is the expected work for a verdict-changed change-driven review.

stronger_arguments.description: Adopt 'more than eighteen' or 'nineteen' orders of magnitude framing using the comoving distance (dC = 2.98 Gpc, ratio = 1.01×10¹⁹, log10 = 19.00 exactly) instead of the angular-diameter distance (dA = 1.59 Gpc, ratio = 5.40×10¹⁸, log10 = 18.73). Both readings are scientifically defensible under standard cosmology; comoving distance is the natural choice for 'where in space the object is' under a comoving-frame interpretation, while angular-diameter is the natural choice for 'what angular size we observe.' A defender-resistant SC numeric prefers the larger, cleaner integer (10^19 exactly vs. ~5×10^18). Adopting 'nineteen orders of magnitude' (using comoving) avoids the slight understatement of 'eighteen' (using dA) and gives WIN-054 a sharp single-number SC anchor: ~10^19 ratio, comparable to WIN-053's 1660:1 and WIN-067's 1430× anchors.
sources: Computed live this run via Hogg 1999 distance formulae at z=0.87 with H0=70, OmegaM=0.3, OmegaL=0.7

stronger_arguments.description: Promote λ_A=4283 Mpc internal contradiction to a second SC horn in detail_verdict_text. Computed live: λ_A = 1.32×10²⁶ m; disc_radius = 2.00×10⁷ m; ratio = 6.6×10¹⁸ ≈ 10^18.82. The dome publishes a cosmological-scale length parameter in its own unified-theory table that exceeds the published disc_radius by ~19 orders of magnitude. This is purely dome-internal (no extragalactic reference needed) and constitutes a SECOND independent SC horn. Adding this would give WIN-054 a two-horn SC argument: (1) cited extragalactic scale (El Gordo at ~10^19 m beyond firmament) AND (2) published cosmological parameter (λ_A at ~10^19 × disc_radius). Both horns are dome-internal commitments. WIN-052 has the parallel observation; keeping the framing symmetric across both cosmological WINs that cite λ_A would help cross-WIN consistency.
sources: data/wins.json:054 detail_evidence ('an unexplained λ_A = 4,283 Mpc redshift scale')
Computed live this run: λ_A=4283 Mpc * 3.086e22 m/Mpc = 1.32e26 m

stronger_arguments.description: Surface the cite-without-endorsing defense explicitly in detail_verdict_text rather than leaving it for the reader to infer. The current foreclosure paragraph implies the closure but does not name the defense. Naming it ('the defense that the dome cites measurements without endorsing the interpretive frame') and closing it ('a measurement of mass and velocity at z=0.87 has no content under any framework that denies the spatial scale at which those quantities are defined') would preempt the strongest dome rebuttal explicitly. This is part of Lens A advocate-mode output — the defender's best move, and the response to it.
sources: Lens A advocate-mode output this run (defense rating 3/5 if not explicitly preempted; would drop to 1/5 with the explicit close)

kernel_of_truth.description: El Gordo at z=0.87 is one of the genuinely striking unresolved tensions for ΛCDM cosmology. The 6.2σ significance (data/wins.json:054 detail_evidence cites Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023 doi 10.3847/1538-4357/ace62a) is a legitimate empirical anomaly; the V_infall ≥ 2,500 km/s requirement from Zhang+ 2015 (data/wins.json:054 detail_evidence cites doi 10.1088/0004-637X/813/2/129) does pose a real challenge for hierarchical structure formation at z = 0.87; and the SIDM exploration in Molnar Birkinshaw 2024 confirms mainstream cosmologists treat this as a serious open problem motivating exotic dark-sector physics. The dome author's instinct to cite a real, peer-reviewed anomaly rather than fabricate one is the responsible-reviewer behavior the dome generally exhibits. NARRATE-CITE: this paragraph cites data/wins.json:054 detail_evidence and detail_extra from this review run.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The El Gordo tension's empirical content lives in the cosmological frame in which it is measured — z=0.87, comoving distance ~3 Gpc, angular-diameter distance ~1.59 Gpc, mass M_200c~2e15 M_sun. None of these quantities is definable under the dome's published bounded geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, dimensional ratio to El Gordo's angular-diameter distance ~5×10¹⁸ — computed live this run). The dome publishes one cosmological parameter, λ_A=4,283 Mpc (data/wins.json:054 detail_evidence), itself ~6.6×10¹⁸ × the published disc_radius, which is a second dome-internal contradiction independent of the El Gordo argument. The kernel-of-truth — El Gordo IS a real ΛCDM tension — does not transfer to the dome because the dome cannot import the numerical content of the citation without also importing the spatial framework it bound itself out of. NARRATE-CITE: this paragraph cites data/wins.json:054 detail_evidence and live computation this run (node integration of Hogg 1999 distances).

advocate_mode.best_defense: The dome model does not commit to El Gordo's physical existence at standard-cosmology distance. Cosmological objects — galaxies, clusters, redshifts — are interpretive constructs within the LCDM paradigm; the WIN cites a within-LCDM tension (Asencio Banik Kroupa 2023's 6.2σ figure was calculated using LCDM-derived masses and structure-formation simulations) to disqualify LCDM, not to endorse LCDM's interpretive frame as physically real under the dome. By analogy: a critic of Ptolemaic astronomy can cite the failure of Ptolemy's equants to predict planetary positions without committing to the equants' physical existence. Therefore there is no self-contradiction: the dome cites the WITHIN-LCDM number that breaks LCDM, without endorsing the LCDM ontology that gives the number its frame. The Self-Contradicted verdict imports an assumption (the WIN is endorsing the cosmological-distance ontology) that the dome does not make.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add to detail_verdict_text after the foreclosure paragraph: 'The defense that the WIN cites El Gordo's measurements without endorsing standard cosmology's interpretive frame fails on its own terms. A measurement of mass M~2×10¹⁵ M_⊙ and velocity ~2,500 km/s at z=0.87 has no numerical content under any framework that denies the spatial and temporal scale on which those quantities are defined. The WIN cannot import the Asencio et al. 6.2σ figure as evidence while disowning the LCDM-internal ontology that gives the figure meaning — without the ontology, the figure is just a number with no physical referent. The dome must either accept the spatial framework (and contradict its published 9,086 km firmament) or reject the spatial framework (and forfeit the citation as evidence). There is no third option that lets it have the citation's evidentiary weight without its ontological cost.' This closes the cite-without-endorsing move explicitly. Class hint: deep-attack (genuine new argument content).

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: All seven related WINs are now Self-Contradicted (verified live by node query of data/wins.json:047/048/052/053/055/067/029). The SC pattern across these WINs is consistent: each invokes the dome's own published geometry (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, lambda_A=4,283 Mpc where applicable) to argue foreclosure-by-own-commitments. The four cosmological WINs (047, 048, 052, 054, 055) form a coherent block: same code_analysis tags (monitoring=none, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false), same SC argument shape (bounded geometry forecloses extragalactic referents). Only WIN-054 currently has the finding-versus-verdict self-anchor (verified live by node query — WIN-047/048/052/055 finding fields do NOT end with 'is Not Demonstrated' or similar prior-verdict self-anchors). This is the explanation for why hole #1 fires for WIN-054 specifically and not for the cohort: WIN-054 is the only one whose original (Not Demonstrated) finding wrote 'is Not Demonstrated' as a verbatim closing sentence that the EXP-295-P4 patch did not touch. Cross-WIN consistency is otherwise tight.

claims_checked: Eighteen orders of magnitude beyond the dome's 9,086 km firmament
lambda_A=4,283 Mpc internal contradiction with disc_radius=20,015 km
Disc diameter 40,030 km (vs prior ~30,000 km wrong value flagged in ISS-1994)
quantitative_verification.computations: Computed live this run (node integration of Hogg 1999 distance formulae at z=0.87 with ΛCDM H_0=70 km/s/Mpc, Ω_M=0.3, Ω_Λ=0.7): comoving distance dC = 2975 Mpc = 9.18×10²⁵ m; angular-diameter distance dA = 1591 Mpc = 4.91×10²⁵ m. Ratio dA/firmament_height (9086 km = 9.086×10⁶ m) = 5.40×10¹⁸ → log10 = 18.73. Ratio dC/firmament_height = 1.01×10¹⁹ → log10 = 19.00. Lambda_A=4283 Mpc * 3.086×10²² m/Mpc = 1.32×10²⁶ m; ratio λ_A/disc_radius (20015 km = 2.002×10⁷ m) = 6.60×10¹⁸ → log10 = 18.82; ratio λ_A/firmament_height = 1.45×10¹⁹ → log10 = 19.16. Disc diameter = 2 * 20015 km = 40,030 km (matches current detail_verdict_text wording, post-ISS-1994 fix).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two minor numeric tightenings flagged in holes_found[2]: (1) text says '~4.6×10²⁵ m' for angular-diameter distance; computed 4.91×10²⁵ m (6% understatement, acceptable for OOM argument but defender-vulnerable); (2) text says 'eighteen orders of magnitude' for the dA-based ratio; computed log10 = 18.73 (closer to 19 than 18). Adopting the comoving distance instead yields log10 = 19.00 exactly, which gives a cleaner SC anchor numeric. Neither discrepancy invalidates the SC argument — both are tightenings, not falsifications.

deeper_analysis: WIN-054.c4 is a verdict-changed change-driven review: the audit flagged the verdict flip (Not Demonstrated → Self-Contradicted), and the post-integration adversarial pass surfaces two NEW majors that EXP-295-P4 did not address because both surfaces are downstream of the patched detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict fields. (1) The finding self-anchor is the same class of bug as c3 hole #1 — a prior-verdict closing sentence left in place when only the label was flipped — but now in finding, the most prominent field (summary-table row, what skim readers see first). The fix mirrors what EXP-295-P4 already did for detail_verdict_text: replace the closing self-anchor with a foreclosure-anchored alternative. (2) The cross-page reference case in build-scripts/generate-html.js:1726 is an architecture-debt surface — hardcoded methodology prose in the build script (not in sections.json), naming WIN-054 specifically. This is the second time this run that hardcoded-prose-in-generate-html.js has surfaced (ISS-2025/2038 found the same architecture pattern in WIN sub_rationale dead pointers); the architectural fix (move methodology prose into sections.json) would close both findings in a principled way. The c3 carry-over holes are all resolved cleanly: detail_verdict_text now anchors on foreclosure with the 'Self-Contradicted is the correct verdict' verbatim line in place of the old 'Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict' anchor; tldr_verdict drops the false dilemma-attribution claim; ISS-2025 (and dup ISS-2038) closed the sub_rationale dead pointer. The SC reading itself is sound on the bounded-foreclosure argument; the new holes are about propagation of that change to downstream surfaces. Stronger-argument suggestions from c3 (lambda_A second SC horn; ~10^19 dimensional anchor via comoving distance) are still available and would tighten the SC argument further. Net: ENDORSE the integrated state; flag two new major propagation bugs that the verdict flip introduced; two minor tightenings available. No verdict change recommended.
